# Feature Proposal: [name]

**Proposed by:** [Mark / Claude / strategist]
**Date:** YYYY-MM-DD
**Status:** Draft / Approved / Rejected / Revised

---

## What & why (2–3 sentences)

[What's the feature? What triggered the proposal? What user behavior does it change?]

---

## 6-point alignment check

### 1. Goal alignment

Which of the 8 goals does this serve? Or, if foundational, which goal-serving features does it enable?

> [Reference goals from PRODUCT-SPEC.md. Be specific — "serves several goals" is too vague.]

### 2. Customer band

Is the benefiting customer in the $300–$800/mo Tier 2 band?

> [If sub-$300 or enterprise, justify why we're stretching the band, or revise.]

### 3. Strategist role

Does the strategist use this, or get leverage from it?

> [Pure-automation that bypasses the strategist is a red flag. Explain why this isn't that.]

### 4. Decline-to-sell risk

Could this feature be used to sell services to owners who aren't ready? If yes — how do we prevent that?

> [Specific: identify the misuse path and the guardrail.]

### 5. NOT violations

Does this cross any explicit NOT-line in the spec? (Channel-first SaaS, marketplace, ops platform, enterprise, generalist.)

> [If yes, justify or revise.]

### 6. Moat compounding

Does this strengthen trust, strategist leverage, or playbook IP? Preferably more than one.

> [If none — is it really a feature, or is it a distraction?]

---

## Verdict

- [ ] **PASSES** — proceed to implementation
- [ ] **FAILS** — concerns:
  - [list]
- [ ] **REVISE** — suggested changes:
  - [list]

---

## Implementation sketch (only if passes)

[High-level approach, ~3–5 bullets. Link to schema migration draft, UX sketch, etc.]

[Anything that needs Mark's call before build starts]
